{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is whether selinexor is effective for multiple myeloma. Reviewing the documents: Document [1] mentions that in cases of triple-class refractory disease, selinexor and dexamethasone are recently approved therapies. Document [3] references the BOSTON Trial, which studied selinexor, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (SVd) as a treatment option for relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma. Document [11] states that in penta-refractory disease, selinexor is one of the therapeutic options. Document [12] notes that selinexor has gastrointestinal toxicity as a side effect, which is consistent with its use in treatment regimens. These references confirm that selinexor is used in clinical treatment settings for multiple myeloma, particularly in refractory cases. Therefore, selinexor is considered effective in the context of multiple myeloma treatment.", "answer_choice": "A"}
